body:
## Why this matters

#54 explicitly names reproducible VM provisioning as a follow-up sourced from `projectbluefin/bluespeed#26`, but the current queue only defines the first substrate lane in #56. Without a dedicated provisioning-contract issue, later storage and workload epics will keep rediscovering how homelab-role VMs are created, rerun, and torn down in this repo's Argo + KubeVirt + GitOps model.

## Scope

Define the reproducible provisioning follow-up under #54:

- translate the `create-vm` idea into this repo's KubeVirt + Argo workflow surface rather than a local libvirt recipe
- define the minimum repo-owned provisioning evidence: named VM creation, boot, reachability, reboot, teardown, rerunnable or idempotent behavior, and emitted outputs/artifacts
- state what belongs in substrate validation versus higher-level storage/service follow-up
- call out any cluster or GitOps prerequisites that must exist before higher-level epics can target the lane

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A reproducible homelab-role VM provisioning contract is defined for this repo's workflow model
- [ ] The issue specifies the minimum lifecycle evidence the provisioning path must emit
- [ ] Rerun/idempotence expectations are explicit
- [ ] Substrate-only scope is separated clearly from storage/service follow-up work

## Links

- Child of #54
- Depends on #56
- Blocks parts of #52 and #51
- Source ideas: `projectbluefin/bluespeed#1`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#26`
title: feat: define reproducible homelab VM provisioning contract for the lab
